channel.title: Microsoft Security News
channel.description: Aggregated Microsoft security news and advisories

item.title: Agent 365 connector: Monitor, hunt, and investigate AI agent activity in Microsoft Sentinel
item.description: As enterprises scale the use of AI agents, SOC teams need visibility into AI agent behavior. The Agent 365 connector, now in public preview, streams rich agent telemetry from Agent 365 into Microsoft Sentinel data lake. Agent activity, such as agent data exposure or access drift, is surfaced...
item.dc:creator: RGupta

item.title: How to better protect your growing business in an AI-powered world
item.description: See how built-in security helps keep your growing business running, protect customer trust, and support growth. The post How to better protect your growing business in an AI-powered world appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Alym Rayani

item.title: Introducing selective response actions for high-value assets in Microsoft Defender
item.description: Deploying Microsoft Defender on high-value assets (HVAs) such as domain controllers, ADFS servers, and other Tier-0 systems, requires a thoughtful approach to balance strong protection with operational stability. Given the powerful response capabilities available, organizations often seek greater...
item.dc:creator: amibarayev

item.title: CVE-2026-32185 Microsoft Teams Spoofing Vulnerability
item.description: The security update for Microsoft Teams for Android is not immediately available. Customers running affected Microsoft Teams for would need to install the update to be protected from this vulnerability, once the update becomes available.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-42897 Microsoft Exchange Server Spoofing Vulnerability
item.description: Updated FAQ information. This is an informational change only.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-42822 Azure Local Disconnected Operations (ALDO) Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
item.description: Improper authentication in Azure Local Disconnected Operations allows an unauthorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-32177 .NET Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
item.description: Update the Security Updates table to remove incorrectly added software
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-43308 btrfs: don't BUG() on unexpected delayed ref type in run_one_delayed_ref()
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Build a Local Microsoft Sentinel Triage Agent in VS Code (Copilot + MCP)
item.description: Modern SOC work is not limited by data—it’s limited by the friction of collecting it. This post shows a local-first workflow that lets you investigate Microsoft Sentinel incidents from inside VS Code using GitHub Copilot Chat for reasoning and a small, deterministic MCP toolset for evidence...
item.dc:creator: absharan

item.title: CVE-2026-8328 FTP PASV SSRF, ftpcp() does not use actual peer address, trusts server-supplied PASV host address
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-8368 LWP::UserAgent versions before 6.83 for Perl leak Authorization and Proxy-Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-7210 The expat and elementtree parsers use insufficient entropy for XML hash-flooding protection
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-44283 etcd: Read access via PrevKv in etcd transactions may bypass RBAC authorization checks
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-46483 Vim: Command injection in tar#Vimuntar via missing shellescape {special} flag
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-44662 rust-openssl: Heap buffer overflow when encrypting with AES key-wrap-with-padding
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-42946 NGINX ngx_http_scgi_module and ngx_http_uwsgi_module vulnerability
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-42934 NGINX ngx_http_charset_module vulnerability
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-40460 NGINX ngx_quic_module vulnerability
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-6477 PostgreSQL libpq lo_* functions let server superuser overwrite client stack memory
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-6637 PostgreSQL refint allows stack buffer overflow and SQL injection
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-6638 PostgreSQL REFRESH PUBLICATION allows SQL injection via table name
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-6473 PostgreSQL server undersizes allocations, via integer wraparound
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-6478 PostgreSQL discloses MD5-hashed passwords via covert timing channel
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CVE-2026-44673 libyang: lyb_read_string() integer overflow → heap buffer overflow
item.description: Information published.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How Microsoft Defender used predictive shielding to proactively disrupt a ransomware attack
item.description: Modern ransomware attacks are increasingly designed to blend in with normal IT operations, using trusted administrative tools to quietly weaken defenses and distribute malicious payloads at scale. In a recent real‑world incident, a human‑operated ransomware actor attempted to do exactly that by...
item.dc:creator: AvivSharon

item.title: Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents
item.description: As AI agents gain autonomy, defense in depth must evolve, with application-layer design, identity, and human oversight at the center. The post Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Ofstein and Elliot H Omiya

item.title: Kazuar: Anatomy of a nation-state botnet
item.description: Kazuar, a sophisticated malware family attributed to the Russian state actor Secret Blizzard, has been under constant development for years and continues to evolve in support of espionage-focused operations. Over time, Kazuar has expanded from a relatively traditional backdoor into a highly modular...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: When configuration becomes a vulnerability: Exploitable misconfigurations in AI apps
item.description: Exposed UIs, weak authentication, and risky defaults could turn cloud-native AI apps on Kubernetes into potential targets by threat actors. Learn how exploitable misconfigurations lead to RCE and data leaks. The post When configuration becomes a vulnerability: Exploitable misconfigurations in AI...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Yossi Weizman

item.title: TLS Certificate Pinning and Best Practices in Azure Open-Source Relational Databases
item.description: TLS certificate pinning in Azure Database for PostgreSQL and MySQL Transport Layer Security (TLS) encrypts data in transit between client applications and the server and authenticates the service endpoint in client-server authentication. Azure Database server certificates are issued by well-known...
item.dc:creator: TameikaL

item.title: Check This Out! (CTO!) Guide (April 2026)
item.description: Member: TysonPaul | Microsoft Community Hub Announcing Public Preview for Essential Machine Management Team Blog: Azure Governance and Management Author: Meagan McCrory Published: 04/06/2026 Summary: Microsoft has announced the public preview of Essential Machine Management within Azure’s Compute...
item.dc:creator: TysonPaul

item.title: Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs generation
item.description: What if you could generate realistic attack telemetry on demand? Explore research methods that translate attacker behaviors (TTPs) into synthetic logs that can trigger detections at scale and without sensitive data. The post Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description: Today Microsoft is announcing a major step forward in AI-powered cyber defense: a new multi-model agentic scanning harness (codenamed MDASH). The post Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Taesoo Kim

item.title: Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks
item.description: Read how to protect consumer websites and defend against modern DDoS attacks with layered security, resilient architecture, and graceful service degradation. The post Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kumar Srinivasamurthy

item.title: Undermining the trust boundary: Investigating a stealthy intrusion through third-party compromise
item.description: Microsoft Incident Response investigated an attack operated through legitimate and trusted administrative mechanisms to blend seamlessly into routine operations and remain undetected demonstrating that intrusions have increasingly avoided using noisy exploits, obvious malware, or custom tooling,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Incident Response

item.title: Triggering Azure Functions from Blob Storage Using Event Grid
item.description: Overview Modern workloads increasingly rely on reacting to files as soon as they arrive in Azure Blob Storage. While Azure provides multiple ways to trigger computing from blob operations, choosing the right event-driven pattern is not always straightforward—especially in enterprise environments...
item.dc:creator: AndrewCoughlin

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: RSAC 2026
item.description: Security is entering a new era, one defined by explosive data growth, increasingly sophisticated threats, and the rise of AI-enabled operations. To keep pace, security teams need an AI-powered approach to collect, reason over, and act on security data at scale. At RSA Conference 2026 (RSAC), we’re...
item.dc:creator: spalani

item.title: Active attack: Dirty Frag Linux vulnerability expands post-compromise risk
item.description: Dirty Frag is a newly disclosed Linux local privilege escalation vulnerability affecting kernel networking and memory-fragment handling components including esp4, esp6, and rxrpc. The vulnerability enables reliable escalation from an unprivileged user to root and may be leveraged after initial...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description: New research exposes how prompt injection in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. Learn how these vulnerabilities work, what’s impacted, and how to secure your agents. The post When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks appeared first on Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team, Uri Oren, Amit Eliahu and Dor Edry

item.title: Better together with Azure WAF + Microsoft Defender for Storage + Defender for Azure SQL Databases
item.description: Authored by: Fernanda_Vela​ , saikishor​, Yura_Lee​ Reviewed by: YuriDiogenes​, Mohit_Kumar​, Amir_Dahan​, eitanbremler​ , Kitt_Weatherman​ Introduction Often, customers ask why additional workload protection is needed when a web application firewall is already in place. Azure Web Application...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Extending Sentinel Data Integration: Azure Blob Storage Support for CCF Connectors
item.description: As organizations scale their security operations, the ability to ingest, process, and analyze high volumes of data reliably becomes increasingly critical. Microsoft Sentinel continues to expand its ecosystem through the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF), enabling ISVs to build and deliver...
item.dc:creator: JesseKopavi

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. April brings a broad set of updates, with RSAC 2026 announcements rolling out alongside new features. Highlights include cost limit enforcement to prevent runaway query costs, curated open-source intelligence in Threat...
item.dc:creator: vkokkengada

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Container runtime anti-malware detection and blocking and DNS Detection for Kubernetes is now GA in Defender for Containers for AKS, EKS, and GKE. Learn more about these announcements here and here. Defender for Storage integration in Azure Portal Storage Center...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Monthly news - May 2026
item.description: Microsoft DefenderMonthly news - May 2026 Edition This is our monthly "What's new" blog post, summarizing product updates and various new assets we released over the past month across our Defender products. In this edition, we are looking at all the goodness from April 2026. We are now including...
item.dc:creator: HeikeRitter

item.title: Purpose For Your PKI (Practical PKI Part 3)
item.description: My name is Ron Arestia, and I am a Security Researcher with Microsoft’s Detection and Response Team (DART). We respond to customer cybersecurity incidents to assist with containment and recovery from threat actors. In this brief blog post, we will discuss the “why” behind your PKI. This is part 3...
item.dc:creator: RonArestia

item.title: Public Preview: Managed Identity support for graphical session recording
item.description: Overview Azure Bastion provides secure RDP and SSH access to Azure virtual machines directly via the Azure portal or via the native SSH/RDP client already installed on your local computer. Today, we are introducing public preview for managed identity support for session recording, giving...
item.dc:creator: aarontsang

item.title: Hardening OpenClaw on AKS: Mitigating Container Escapes with Kata microVM Isolation
item.description: What is OpenClaw, and what security challenges does it pose with container escapes? OpenClaw is an open-source autonomous AI agent designed for power users and developers to automate tasks, such as managing emails, files, and scheduling via chat apps like WhatsApp or Telegram. While OpenClaw...
item.dc:creator: jianshn

item.title: Use Data Wrangler to Streamline Your Microsoft Sentinel data lake Notebook Development
item.description: One of the many exciting features of the Microsoft Sentinel data lake is a built-in advanced analytics engine, powered by Apache Spark. This Spark cluster has access to data that is within Sentinel data lake, and can work with this data through Jupyter notebooks in Visual Studio Code. As with any...
item.dc:creator: David Hoerster

item.title: Granular email content access with unified RBAC – now the default for new Defender tenants
item.description: Email investigations are a key part of detecting and responding to phishing and malware. As security workflows continue to evolve, there is an increasing need to align email content visibility more closely with specific roles and scenarios, such as Tier‑1 analysis or specialized workflows like...
item.dc:creator: VipulPandey

item.title: How to Manage RC4 Hardening – Definitive Guide
item.description: How to Manage RC4 Hardening – Definitive Guide This article is a technical continuation of the RC4 deprecation / Kerberos hardening work I covered in my previous article last month. If you already went through the “why” (risk of RC4, what changes Microsoft is rolling out, and the high-level...
item.dc:creator: Elanor92

item.title: General availability of Default Ruleset (DRS) 2.2 for Web Application Firewall
item.description: Introduction As attackers continue to evolve their techniques, organizations require web application security that keeps pace with emerging threats without disrupting legitimate traffic. Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) continues to evolve to meet these demands and now supports Default Rule Set...
item.dc:creator: andrewmathu

item.title: Monthly news - January 2026
item.description: Microsoft DefenderMonthly news - January 2026 Edition This is our monthly "What's new" blog post, summarizing product updates and various new assets we released over the past month across our Defender products. In this edition, we are looking at all the goodness from December 2025. Defender for...
item.dc:creator: HeikeRitter

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2025: Transforming Phishing Response with Agentic Innovation
item.description: Phishing attacks remain one of the most persistent and damaging threats to organizations worldwide. Security teams are under constant pressure to investigate a growing number of user reported phishing emails daily, ensuring accurate verdicts and timely responses. As threats grow in volume and...
item.dc:creator: JeffreyPinkston

item.title: Microsoft Defender: New Advanced hunting enhancements
item.description: Co-author: Jeremy Tan As a security analyst who actively hunts for critical threats, one of the most frustrating things that can happen is hitting a limit mid-query or encounter an experience that doesn’t behave as expected. The resulting friction and time spent troubleshooting or navigating takes...
item.dc:creator: Noa_Nutkevitch

item.title: Extracting and Auditing Azure DevOps Permissions at Scale with PowerShell
item.description: Introduction Azure DevOps organizations accumulate permissions over time. Groups are created, users are added, Entra (Azure AD) groups are nested into project groups, and team structures evolve. For organizations subject to compliance requirements, security reviews, or simply wanting to understand...
item.dc:creator: skissel

item.title: Assess Secure Boot status with Microsoft Defender
item.description: Understanding the Secure Boot certificate challenge Secure Boot is a foundational security feature that validates the integrity of your device's boot process, ensuring only trusted software can run during system startup. This protection has been quietly defending enterprise devices since 2012, but...
item.dc:creator: amitcohen

item.title: Designing Outbound Connectivity for "Private Subnets" in Azure
item.description: Why Private Subnets Change Everything Historically, Azure virtual machines relied on default outbound internet access, where the platform automatically assigned a dynamic SNAT IP from a shared pool. This was convenient but problematic: ❌ No deterministic outbound IP addresses ❌ No traffic...
item.dc:creator: alexeyn1

item.title: Introducing the Microsoft Sentinel Training Lab. Hands-On Security Operations in Minutes
item.description: A huge thanks to Paul Kew - this lab wouldn't have been possible without his contributions. Security operations is one of those things that’s hard to learn from slides alone. You need to feel what it’s like to triage a multi-stage incident, tune a noisy detection rule, or trace an attacker pivoting...
item.dc:creator: AndreasKapetaniou

item.title: Declutter and Defend: Reducing promotional mail noise with Microsoft Defender
item.description: Enterprise inboxes are overwhelmed with graymail — legitimate, bulk email like newsletters, vendor promotions, and product updates that isn't malicious but buries the messages that matter. When high volumes of these mails land in the inbox, it crowds out priority communications and can dull...
item.dc:creator: FaithEbenezerOquong

item.title: Microsoft Sentinel MCP Server with external AI models (Claude) for natural language investigations
item.description: Security teams are increasingly exploring how AI assistants support them in investigating incidents, asking questions, and exploring their data. At the same time, controlling how data is accessed remains critical. Today, we’re sharing how Sentinel can support a third-party AI assistant like Claude...
item.dc:creator: mcasgrain

item.title: Security Copilot for SOC: bringing agentic AI to every defender
item.description: Cybersecurity has entered an era of relentless complexity. As threat actors increasingly leverage artificial intelligence to automate attacks, evade detection, and scale their tactics, defenders are challenged to keep up. In this new era, security operations centers (SOCs) must transform to not...
item.dc:creator: cristinadagamah

item.title: Strengthening Identity Resilience: A Deep Dive into Microsoft Entra Backup and Recovery
item.description: In the modern security landscape, we often say that "Identity is the new perimeter." We spend significant resources on Conditional Access, Phishing-Resistant MFA, and Identity Protection to keep the "bad guys" out. But what happens when the threat is already inside, or when a legitimate...
item.dc:creator: Farooque

item.title: Running multimedia AI models on Container Apps with Serverless GPU (A100 & T4)
item.description: A video format is available for watching. Prerequisites - An Azure account with sufficient permissions to create resources. - Terraform installed on your local machine. Infrastructure Provisioning Clone the Github repository and navigate to the project directory. Initialize Terraform and apply the...
item.dc:creator: HoussemDellai

item.title: What Changed in RC4 with the January 2026 Windows Update and Why it is Important
item.description: In case you haven’t heard, RC4 is not secure and has been deprecated. In this article, I will discuss what changed with the January 2026 Windows Update and why it is important to start auditing and remediate RC4 usage is your environment. Starting with the January 13, 2026, Windows security...
item.dc:creator: Elanor92
